title: Risk Management
partialNote: This is a partial chapter assignment. Read only the sections listed below. Risk management model comparisons and third-party risk frameworks are omitted — focus on the assessment and prioritization workflow you will apply in Lab 5.2.
readSections: What Is Risk Management? — Risk Response Techniques and Risk Management Frameworks only
Business Risks (Business Impact Analysis, Mission-Essential Functions, Identification of Critical Systems, Single Point of Failure, Impact)
Quantitatively Assessing Risk (Adding Objectivity to a Qualitative Assessment, Risk Calculation)
Risk Management Best Practices (System Vulnerabilities, Threat Vectors, Probability/Threat Likelihood, Risks Associated with Cloud Computing and Virtualization)
skipSections: An Overview of Risk Management — Example of Risk Management at the International Banking Level
What Is Risk Management? — Risk Management Culture
Business Risks — Examples of Business Risks, Examples of Technology Risks
Third-party Risks (Vendor Management, Supply Chain, Outsourced Code Development, Data Storage)
Risk Mitigation Strategies (Change Management, Incident Management, User Rights and Permissions Reviews, Data Loss or Theft)
Risk Management Models (General Risk Management Model, Software Engineering Institute Model, NIST Risk Models, Model Application)
focusAreas: Pay special attention to Qualitatively Assessing Risk and Business Impact Analysis. The Risk Matrix/Heat Map is the primary tool you will build in Lab 5.2, and the Business Risks section explains how to translate technical findings into language Amanda Nguyen can take to her investors. Risk Matrix and Probability/Threat Likelihood are Security+ exam staples.
secplus: Domain 5.2 (Risk Management Processes), Domain 5.3 (Risk Mitigation Strategies), Domain 3.4 (Resilience and Recovery)
